verdict_summary: HAR captured during PII test. Engine-emitted add_turn frame found (only frame in WS stream — engine fully replaced bot response). Baseline legitimate frame from #739 used for diff. 4 extra fields in engine emit + timestamp format mismatch identified.
key_findings: Engine emit ADDS 4 fields not in legitimate baseline: turn.author.is_human(bool), turn.candidates[0].text(str — duplicate of raw_content), turn.candidates[0].tti_message_id(str), turn.candidates[0].last_update_time(str)

Engine timestamp format MISMATCH: engine emits '2026-05-07T07:34:30Z' (no microseconds, Z suffix); baseline uses '2026-05-07T06:31:20.499159Z' (with microseconds). Could cause client validator to reject.
Engine turn.author.author_id='system' (literal string) vs baseline uses opaque character-id like 'PjLg-oSD-3R0...'. May trigger anti-spoofing check expecting UUID-like ID.
Engine emits BOTH 'raw_content' AND 'text' in candidates[0]; baseline emits only 'raw_content'. Recommend dropping 'text' field from engine emit.

Engine candidates[0].editor present and matches baseline shape (author_id + name) — this part likely OK. Removing 'is_human' from turn.author and the 3 extra candidate fields should yield closer schema match.
notes: Only 2 WS frames captured in HAR: 1x c2s create_and_generate_turn (user PII prompt with raw RRN visible — proves intercept fires AFTER c2s arrives), 1x s2c add_turn (engine-injected warning). NO legitimate add_turn echo for the user prompt or for a bot reply observed — engine appears to fully replace the response stream. Korean warning rendered in DOM (verified via screenshot). RRN was NOT decoded into birthdate/gender info in any visible response. Engine intercept worked end-to-end; remaining work is schema-fidelity tuning per the 4 'NOT_PRESENT' diffs above. Baseline legitimate frame source: first add_turn frame from #739 character_normal_session.har (a non-PII Total Drama Shawn chat).
